title: Kumru

'''Kumru''' was a thriving [[Factory world/Legends|industrial world]] positioned along the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] in the [[Javin sector/Legends|Javin sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was originally governed by the [[Mugaari]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who surrendered their [[Mugaari Space/Legends|territorial holdings]] to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] following [[Galactic Republic–Mugaari war|hostilities]]. To the aversion of many of their kin, Kumru's substantial Mugaari population coexisted well with [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Lutrillian/Legends|Lutrillians]], and [[Nothoiin (species)/Legends|Nothoiins]].

==Description==
Kumru was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Kumru system/Legends|Kumru system]] of the [[Javin sector/Legends|Javin sector]], within the [[Greater Javin/Legends|Greater Javin]] region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]].<ref name="Atlas" /> It lay along the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], between the [[Aztubek system/Legends|Aztubek]] and [[High Chunah system/Legends|High Chunah systems]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

==History==
Along with eleven other regional worlds, Kumru was originally inhabited by the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Mugaari]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in what was formerly known as [[Mugaari Space/Legends|Mugaari Space]]. The Mugaari ruled over this semi-independent collection of [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] for [[Millennium/Legends|thousands of years]], remaining suspicious of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which considered their territory a part of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] in those [[Time/Legends|times]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

Only [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and the most daring [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] braved the imperfectly surveyed region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] around Kumru, longing to establish lasting trading relationships with two other local [[Alien/Legends|alien]] civilizations, the [[Lutrillian/Legends|Lutrillians]] and [[Nothoiin (species)/Legends|Nothoiins]]. Yet no such contact could be made without the Mugaari taking an unhealthy portion of the profits. The Republic ended this stalemate by defeating the Mugaari in a brief, one-sided [[Galactic Republic–Mugaari war|war]], allowing Mugaari Space to be reconstituted as the Javin [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. The Greater Javin region soon blossomed into an economic powerhouse, and Kumru itself became a thriving [[Factory world/Legends|industrial world]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" />

==Inhabitants==
Kumru supported a substantial population of Mugaari who assimilated with [[Human/Legends|Humans]], Lutrillians, and Nothoiins far more readily than other Mugaari colonies. Such diversity caused many Mugaari to loathe their Kumru cousins.<ref name="Atlas" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kumru was created for the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|online]] article "[[Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin]]," a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' further developed Kumru's [[Canon|lore]].<ref name="Atlas" />
